lastCommit.message: Ingest 183 DM answers across the Aestaria continent: dragon roster (Vulcaris, Argentyr, Cadmarre, Cupraxis, Aurethys, Saturnyx, Platyr), Guardian cosmology, inverted economy, all 13 regions + 5 cities; new Tarnish + Sunderage pages; consolidate 74 duplicate answer cards; polish stale NPC names

barRaise.topFinding: BLOCK: a data surface that the DM gate is supposed to cover is reachable without the gate, so content flagged secret: true is being served to readers who should not have it. The page-level gate itself works; the defect is that the deploy-time check only ever proves the page gate and never exercises the data path behind it, which is why this went unnoticed. Verified live this run by comparing identifier sets only, with no secret text read out. Specifics are deliberately withheld from this file: status JSON is a public surface and the hole is still open. Full evidence and the fix are in the local gitignored report at docs/BAR_RAISE-2026-07-16.md. Nine of twelve dimensions are strong (dependency 96, strategic 95, cost 92, solo-tool-ux 90, data-integrity 88), so health lands at 80; the BLOCK gates the verdict without rewriting scores.

actions.title: Close the gated-data exposure: scope the shared store so gated content is not served on the open path, and require the gate on its export. See the local report.
Extend the release check past the page gate: assert the data path carries no gated identifiers for an anonymous caller.
Cap field lengths on the unauthenticated write paths and stop accepting the privileged author name without the gate cookie.
Teach the build that a public page linking a gated URL is a broken link.
Log KV write failures instead of swallowing them, and assert payloads rather than status codes.
Serve the public question board from a cached projection instead of walking the whole answer store on every load.
Hoist one esc() into a shared client preamble and delete the six divergent copies in web/build.mjs.
Add hard_rules and hard_rule_checks to .project-context.json so the BLOCK gate has something to check against.

deferred.title: The DM gate password literal sits in three tracked files in the private repo.
One character carries secret: true while being named on two public pages; the flag or the references has to give.
PROJECT_SPEC never records the item catalog's open5e/OGL provenance.
